description: Adversarial CBOR byte sequences that are structurally valid CBOR (or valid-looking prefixes) but must be rejected by the strict canonical KernelRecord decoder (docs/KrakenKernelSpecification.md §2.3): non-minimal integer/length encodings, unsorted or duplicate map keys, float64 values, tagged values, indefinite-length collections, trailing bytes, truncated input, and integers outside the JS safe-integer range. Every case is a byte-for-byte deviation from the canonical deterministic CBOR encoding kernel spec §2.3 requires; each implementation's strict decoder must refuse to assign these bytes a durable record identity.

non-minimal-uint-head.description: Encodes the small unsigned integer 5 using a 1-byte-argument major-type-0 head (0x18 0x05) instead of the canonical minimal single-byte head (0x05); canonical CBOR requires the shortest-possible integer encoding.

non-minimal-length-head.description: Encodes a 1-element array using a 1-byte-argument length head (0x98 0x01) instead of the canonical minimal short-form length head (0x81); canonical CBOR requires the shortest-possible length encoding.

unsorted-map-keys.description: Encodes the map {"b": 1, "a": 2} with keys in insertion order rather than the canonical bytewise-lexicographic key order ("a" before "b") that deterministic CBOR requires.

duplicate-map-key.description: Encodes a 2-entry map that repeats the same key ("a") twice; canonical KernelRecord maps must have unique string keys.

float64-value.description: Encodes the value 1.0 as an 8-byte IEEE-754 float64 (major type 7, additional info 27); the canonical KernelRecord number domain excludes floating-point representations entirely.

tagged-value.description: Wraps the text string "a" in CBOR tag 0 (major type 6); KernelRecord values must decode to one of the supported primitive/array/map kinds with no tag wrapper.

indefinite-length-array.description: Encodes a 2-element array [1, 2] using the indefinite-length array head (0x9f) terminated by the break byte (0xff) instead of the canonical definite-length head; deterministic CBOR forbids indefinite-length collections.

indefinite-length-map.description: Encodes the single-entry map {"a": 1} using the indefinite-length map head (0xbf) terminated by the break byte (0xff) instead of the canonical definite-length head; deterministic CBOR forbids indefinite-length collections.

indefinite-length-text-string.description: Encodes the text string "a" as an indefinite-length text-string chunk stream (0x7f ... 0xff) instead of a single definite-length text string; deterministic CBOR forbids indefinite-length text.

trailing-bytes-after-top-level-value.description: A complete, canonical encoding of the integer 1 (0x01) followed by one extra trailing byte (0x02); a canonical KernelRecord byte string must contain exactly one top-level value with no trailing data.

truncated-input.description: A 1-entry map head declaring a text-string key "a" but with the corresponding value byte missing entirely; the byte stream ends before the value the map header promises.

uint-beyond-safe-integer-range.description: Encodes the maximum unsigned 64-bit integer (2^64 - 1) via an 8-byte-argument major-type-0 head; this exceeds Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER and the KernelRecord number domain requires every integer to be a JS-safe integer.
